'''Jonathan Charles Neill''' (born 19 December 1968) is a former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 1980s, 1990s and 2000s. He played at representative level for [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland]] and [[Cumbria rugby league team|Cumbria]], and at club level for [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]], [[Huddersfield Giants]] and [[Swinton Lions]], as a {{rlp|pr}}.<ref>{{cite book|first1= Graham|last1= Williams|first2= Peter|last2= Lush|first3= David|last3= Farrar|title= The British Rugby League Records Book|publisher= London League|isbn= 978-1-903659-49-6|year= 2009|pages= 106–107}}</ref>

==Background==
Jon Neill's birth was registered in [[Whitehaven]], [[Cumberland]], England.

==Playing career==
===St Helens===
Neill was signed by [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] from junior club [[Kells A.R.L.F.C.|Kells]] in 1987. He established a regular first team place during the [[1990–91 Rugby Football League season|1990–91 season]] and played in the [[1990–91 Challenge Cup]] final, losing 8–13 against [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]].

Neill played {{rlp|pr}} in St Helens' 24–14 victory over [[Rochdale Hornets]] in the [[1991–92 Lancashire Cup]] final during the [[1991–92 Rugby Football League season|1991–92 season]] on Sunday 20 October 1991. He also played in the [[1991–92 Rugby League Premiership|1991–92 Premiership]] final at the end of the season, but lost 16–48 against Wigan.

In the following season, Neill played in the [[1992–93 Rugby League Premiership|1992–93 Premiership]] final, but was on the winning side this time with a 10–4 victory against Wigan.

Neill did not play for Saints during the [[1995–96 Rugby Football League season|1995–96 season]] due to a contract dispute, as he was reluctant to become a full-time professional.<ref>{{cite web |title=Eric Hughes talks to Denis Whittle |url=https://www.lancashiretelegraph.co.uk/news/6215705.eric-hughes-talks-denis-whittle/ |website=Lancashire Telegraph |access-date=14 February 2022 |date=30 November 1995}}</ref> He returned to the first team during the inaugural [[Super League I|Super League]] season, playing in a 60–16 win against [[Workington Town]] on 16 June 1996.<ref>{{cite web |title=Denis Whittle talk with Shaun McRae |url=https://www.lancashiretelegraph.co.uk/news/6203104.denis-whittle-talk-shaun-mcrae/ |website=Lancashire Telegraph |access-date=14 February 2022 |date=20 June 1996}}</ref>

===Later career===
In 1996, Neill joined [[Huddersfield Giants]] on a free transfer.<ref>{{cite web |title=Scott signs off for Saints |url=https://www.lancashiretelegraph.co.uk/news/6200336.scott-signs-off-saints/ |website=Lancashire Telegraph |access-date=14 February 2022 |date=1 August 1996}}</ref>

Neill finished his playing career with [[Swinton Lions]]. In 2001, he suffered a cruciate ligament injury and was ruled out for the rest of the season.<ref>{{cite web |title=Holdstock aims for home rule |url=https://www.lancashiretelegraph.co.uk/news/6041185.holdstock-aims-home-rule/ |website=Lancashire Telegraph |access-date=14 February 2022 |date=9 March 2001}}</ref> He announced his retirement in 2002.<ref>{{cite web |title=Nanyn Out For Doncaster Trip |url=https://www.lancashiretelegraph.co.uk/news/5982451.nanyn-doncaster-trip/ |website=Lancashire Telegraph |publisher=Newsquest Media Group |access-date=14 February 2022 |date=1 March 2002}}</ref>

===Representative honours===
Neill won two caps for [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland]] in 1997–1999 while at Huddersfield Giants.<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/> He also represented Cumbria in 1994 in a match against [[Australia national rugby league team|Australia]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Kangaroos Tour 1994 Series |url=https://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/matches/kangaroos-tour-1994/game-1/cumbria-vs-australia.html |website=Rugby League Project |access-date=14 February 2022}}</ref>
